title: Active Inference
subtitle: A framework for understanding perception, action, learning, cognition, and adaptive systems.
audience: Newcomers, learners, researchers, builders, and collaborators.
lede: Active Inference is the central scientific and practical framework around which the Institute organizes education, research, applications, and ecosystem support.

sections.heading: What Active Inference Provides
sections.body: Active Inference connects perception, action, uncertainty, and model-based adaptation. The Institute presents it as a shared framework for scientific inquiry, engineering practice, education, and collective sensemaking.

sections.heading: How to Approach the Field
sections.body: Newcomers can begin with the ecosystem reference, the Start repository, public livestreams, podcasts, learning groups, readings, and implementations. The site separates those entry points into stable public pathways.

sections.heading: Implementations
sections.body: Implementation pathways include RxInfer.jl, PyMDP, SPM, symbolic cognitive robotics, notational tools, and domain-specific repositories. These support modeling, simulation, inference, and applied experimentation.

sections.heading: Applications
sections.body: Active Inference appears across domains including biology, neuroscience, mental health, robotics, education, economics, physics, social systems, logistics, decentralized science, and scientific method.

cards.title: Framework
cards.text: A shared language for perception, action, uncertainty, and adaptive systems.

cards.title: Learning path
cards.text: Textbook groups, courses, streams, podcasts, readings, and shared learning repositories.

cards.title: Practice
cards.text: Software implementations, model-building, project work, and applied examples.

cards.title: Ecosystem
cards.text: A broad community applying the framework across scientific and practical domains.
